strengths: Deep production TypeScript/Node.js and React experience across multiple Berlin companies (Optilyz, RIDE Capital, re:cinq)
Covers several nice-to-haves: LLM/MCP agent tooling at re:cinq, AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes deployment of his own platform
Mission-adjacent background: founded an open-source geo-data platform used for environmental/green mapping since 2016
concerns: Severely overqualified: roughly 19 years of experience and a current Staff Engineer title against an entry-level IC role — strong risk of compensation mismatch, disengagement, and early attrition
Core stack gaps for this team: PostgreSQL and Nest.js appear nowhere on the resume; recent database depth is MongoDB, with relational exposure limited to generic SQL/MySQL mentions

Currently employed (re:cinq, April 2025 — present) while also running GISCollective as founder — motivation for a junior, high on-site position is unexplained and his attention is structurally divided

key_factors.factor: Unexplained motivation: active Staff role plus ongoing founder commitment
No evidenced PostgreSQL or Nest.js experience

response.justification: The candidate can plainly clear the technical bar of a junior/mid fullstack role, but that is the core problem: a Staff-level engineer with nearly two decades of experience, a current job, and an active company of his own is a poor structural fit for an entry-level, high on-site individual-contributor position. Compensation expectations, growth trajectory, and day-to-day scope are all misaligned, making early attrition or quiet disengagement the most likely outcome, and the role's stated aim of developing someone into progressively more responsibility is wasted here. Meanwhile the team's named tools — PostgreSQL and Nest.js — are unevidenced, so despite his seniority he would still ramp on the core stack. For this specific opening he is a weak fit.
